steps.title: Identify the effect of omission
steps.content: The text says the musical portrays Hamilton as a compelling, well-defined protagonist but leaves out beliefs that would make him ideologically complex. Omitting those details makes the portrayal clearer and less internally complicated.

steps.title: Reject unsupported effects
steps.content: Choice A discusses overlooked contributions, which the text does not mention. Choice B invents a goal of encouraging imitation. Choice D invents a challenge to historical consensus. The effect is a simpler, clearer thematic portrayal. The answer is C.
